label: Stripe
description: Read payments, subscriptions, invoices, and customer data from your Stripe account.

form.title: Connect Stripe
form.subtitle: Paste a Stripe Restricted API key to give Anton scoped, least-privilege access to your account.

methods.label: Restricted API key
methods.description: Paste a Stripe Restricted key (rk_live_… or rk_test_…) scoped to only the resources Anton needs. Safer than a Secret key — permissions are granular and the key can be rolled independently.
methods.how_to:
## Create a Stripe Restricted API key

Restricted keys let you grant Anton read access to exactly the resources it needs — nothing more. Never paste your full Secret key (sk_live_…).

### Steps

1. Log in to the [Stripe Dashboard](https://dashboard.stripe.com/).
2. Go to **Developers → API keys**.
3. Click **Create restricted key**.
4. Give it a name like `Anton`.
5. Under **Permissions**, enable **Read** for the resources you want Anton to access. Common choices:
   - Customers
   - Charges
   - Payment Intents
   - Subscriptions
   - Invoices
   - Products & Prices
6. Click **Create key**. Stripe shows the key once — copy it immediately.
7. Paste the key (starts with `rk_live_` for production or `rk_test_` for test mode) into the field below.

### Notes

- The key is shown **only once**. If you lose it, delete it in the Dashboard and create a new one.
- Use `rk_test_…` keys during setup so no live data is touched.
- You can roll or revoke the key from **Developers → API keys** at any time without affecting other integrations.
- If Anton reports permission errors, return to the key settings and add Read access for the missing resource.

fields.name: restricted_key
fields.label: Restricted API key
fields.description: Create a Restricted key in Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys. Never use your Secret key (sk_live_…).

fields.name: account_name
fields.label: Account nickname
fields.description: Optional label to identify this Stripe connection in Anton.

fields.name: mode
fields.label: Mode
fields.description: Match this to the key you pasted — test keys (rk_test_…) only return test-mode data.

methods.label: Secret API key (not recommended)
methods.description: Use your full Stripe Secret key (sk_live_…). Grants unrestricted access to your entire Stripe account — only use this if you cannot create a Restricted key.
methods.how_to:
## Using a Stripe Secret API key

Secret keys grant full access to your Stripe account and should be avoided in favour of Restricted keys wherever possible.

### Steps

1. Log in to the [Stripe Dashboard](https://dashboard.stripe.com/).
2. Go to **Developers → API keys**.
3. Under **Standard keys**, find the **Secret key** row and click **Reveal live key** (or copy the test key for `sk_test_…`).
4. Copy the key and paste it into the field below.

### Notes

- Secret keys start with `sk_live_` (production) or `sk_test_` (test mode).
- A Secret key has full read and write access to your entire Stripe account including payouts and refunds. Prefer Restricted keys.
- Store this key securely — do not share it or commit it to source control.
- You can roll the key from the Dashboard if you suspect it has been exposed.

fields.name: secret_key
fields.label: Secret API key

fields.label: Mode
fields.description: Match this to the key you pasted — test keys (sk_test_…) only return test-mode data.
